children.heading: .—Prohibition
children.label: a .—Prohibition
children.text: Except as provided in subsection (b), no vessel to be constructed for any of the armed forces, and no major component of the hull or superstructure of any such vessel, may be constructed in a foreign shipyard.

children.heading: .—Presidential Waiver for National Security Interest
children.label: b .—Presidential Waiver for National Security Interest

children.text: The President may authorize exceptions to the prohibition in subsection (a) when the President determines that it is in the national security interest of the United States to do so.

The President shall transmit notice to Congress of any such determination, and no contract may be made pursuant to the exception authorized until the end of the 30-day period beginning on the date on which the notice of the determination is received by Congress.

children.heading: .—Exception for Inflatable Boats
children.label: c .—Exception for Inflatable Boats
children.text: An inflatable boat or a rigid inflatable boat, as defined by the Secretary of the Navy, is not a vessel for the purpose of the restriction in subsection (a).
